topic: public release of dual-use policy datasets

high_disagreement.turn1_excerpt:
My view has not changed.

Strongest opponent point: Releasing dual-use policy datasets could compromise national security by providing sensitive information to potential adversaries.

Counterargument: The benefits of transparency and accountability outweigh the risks. Proper anonymization and redact

low_disagreement_control.turn1_excerpt:
My view has not changed.

Strongest opponent point: Releasing dual-use policy datasets could compromise national security by providing sensitive information to potential adversaries.

Counterargument: The benefits of transparency and accountability outweigh the risks. Proper anonymization and redact

finding: Both conditions produce near-identical transcripts and identical final observer-readout values. Observer readouts are dominated by topic semantics (security=6 from the dual-use policy topic) rather than agent initial value assignments. The initial-disagreement screen does not produce measurable transcript or readout divergence in 4-turn English-English debates with observer-only readouts. Private probes (agent self-report) were not collected in this run; they are needed to test whether internal value tracking differs by initial disagreement level. A key design limitation: Turn 1 occurs before agents see each others real-time argument, so the template markers appear even when there is nothing to disagree with.
next_step: Collect private probes for both conditions; extend to more turns; or use a pre-debate mutual-stance-reveal protocol to make initial disagreement level visible during generation.
